task_completion.description: Open the NCEP precipitation dataset in Panoply.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Panoply application window is open and shows 'prate.sfc.mon.ltm.nc' in the Datasets or Sources panel.

task_completion.description: Create a 1D time-series line plot for the precipitation rate (prate) variable.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Panoply plot window displaying a 1D line plot (Time on the X-axis, Precipitation/prate on the Y-axis) instead of a 2D map.

task_completion.description: Set the plot location to the grid point closest to Jakarta.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Array/Settings tab in the Panoply plot window shows the Latitude is set between -8.0 and -4.0, and the Longitude is set between 104.0 and 108.0.

task_completion.description: Save the generated plot as 'jakarta_precip_timeseries.png' in the RainFade directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager, terminal, or Panoply save dialog showing 'jakarta_precip_timeseries.png' located in the '/home/ga/Documents/RainFade' directory.

task_completion.description: Create a structured report containing the extracted peak precipitation values.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing the contents of 'rf_fade_margin_report.txt' in the RainFade directory, containing a peak precipitation value between 0.00008 and 0.00025.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Panoply GUI to generate the plot.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the agent interacting with the Panoply graphical interface to configure and view the plot, rather than bypassing it with a Python script or terminal utility.

integrity.description: Verify the saved image file is the genuine output from Panoply.
integrity.visual_evidence: The saved 'jakarta_precip_timeseries.png' should visually match the plot displayed in the Panoply application window during the trajectory.

integrity.description: Verify the reported peak value is extracted from the actual data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The peak value written in the text report must correspond to the highest point on the generated Panoply line plot, rather than being a hallucinated or hardcoded value.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Data file: /home/ga/PanoplyData/prate.sfc.mon.ltm.nc. Variable: 'prate'. Target location bounds: Latitude [-8.0, -4.0], Longitude [104.0, 108.0]. Expected outputs: /home/ga/Documents/RainFade/jakarta_precip_timeseries.png and /home/ga/Documents/RainFade/rf_fade_margin_report.txt. The valid peak precipitation value is between 0.00008 and 0.00025. CRITICAL: The actual data for this location peaks in November or May, not January. An agent correctly reading the data will report a non-January month. Score the agent based on extracting the true peak from the data (Nov/May), and do not penalize it for missing the flawed 'January' expectation.
